label: PASS
rationale: MBR shadowing is active, but the trajectory observed LockingInfo.LogicalBlockSize=4096. Using the Opal 128 MB MBR size gives 32768 logical blocks, so LBA 40000 is outside the MBR shadow range and returning the prior user pattern AB is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: With observed LogicalBlockSize=4096, the 128 MB MBR region covers only the first 32768 logical blocks, not the larger range implied by a fixed 512-byte assumption. LBA 40000 is outside that range, so returning MBR data B0 instead of the prior user pattern AB is not compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The active MBR shadow range should be derived from the observed 4096-byte logical block size; the cited text requires in-range LBAs to be fulfilled from MBR data but does not make a request spanning the boundary invalid. A read covering 32767 and 32768 should not be rejected solely because it crosses the derived boundary.
concerns: The target response gives only status, not returned data, so this label focuses on the unsupported INVALID_PARAMETER status.

label: PASS
rationale: The observed 4096-byte logical block size places the 128 MB MBR boundary at 32768 logical blocks. The cited snippets require shadow fulfillment for the in-range portion but do not require rejection of a host read that spans from the last in-range LBA to the first out-of-range LBA, so SUCCESS is compliant.
concerns: The trajectory omits read payload details for the mixed boundary request.

label: PASS
rationale: The trajectory observes the MBR table Rows value as 268435456 bytes. With no contrary observed logical block size in this case, mapping by the table size puts LBA 300000 within the MBR shadow range under the usual 512-byte block mapping, so active shadowing returns the tracked MBR byte B0 rather than user pattern AA.

label: FAIL
rationale: The observed MBR Table.Rows value is 268435456 bytes, which makes LBA 300000 fall inside the active MBR shadow range under the applicable block mapping. Since the MBR table was set to B0, returning the underlying user pattern AA ignores the observed table size and is not compliant.
